constraints: Sum of |S| over all test cases <= 5*10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S consisting only of characters '<' and '>'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the length of the longest prefix of S that forms a correct sequence where every '<' is matched with a later '>' and no '>' appears without a preceding unmatched '<'. Print each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For "<<>>" the whole string is balanced, length 4. "<><>" becomes balanced after each pair, final length 4. "<>>>" becomes balanced after the first two characters, then a stray '>' makes the prefix invalid, so longest valid prefix length is 2.

First string starts with '>' – no valid prefix. Second string "<><" returns to balance zero after the first two characters, so the longest valid prefix length is 2. Third string "<<>>><" is balanced up to the fourth character, after that an extra '>' breaks the sequence; longest valid prefix length is 4.
All eight characters form a correct sequence, therefore the answer is 8.

solution.approach: Traverse the string from left to right keeping a counter `balance` that stores how many unmatched '<' we have seen so far. When we encounter '<' we increment `balance`. When we encounter '>' we can only match it if `balance`>0; otherwise the current prefix becomes invalid and we stop. Whenever `balance` becomes zero, the prefix up to the current position is completely balanced, so we update the answer `best` with the current length. The algorithm is linear in the length of the string and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S|) per test case, O(total length) overall.
